title: drymoglossum acrostichoides
intro: Drymoglossum acrostichoides, also known as the "Indian pipe club moss," is a rare, epiphytic fern that belongs to the family Drymogiaceae. It is native to tropical regions and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Drymoglossum acrostichoides include the potential treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
